name: get_index_info
description: Get comprehensive statistics about the indexed codebase (Python, Go, Java). Use this FIRST to understand the project scope before making other queries.

name: find_symbol
description: Search and filter symbols by name and/or type. Supports Python, Go, and Java. Supports partial matching. Results are paginated.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Symbol name to find. Optional. Can be: short name ('login'), partial name ('auth'), or FQN ('myapp.auth.login')

arguments.name: type
arguments.desc: Filter by single symbol type. Optional. One of: function_definition, method, constructor, property, special_method, class_definition, interface, enum, dataclass, module_variable, constant, class_field, parameter

arguments.name: types
arguments.desc: Filter by multiple symbol types. Optional. Array of type strings. Alternative to 'type' parameter

arguments.name: module
arguments.desc: Filter by module. Optional. Matches symbols whose FQN starts with the module path (e.g., 'core.settings', 'data_manager.models'). Works with all symbol types

arguments.name: limit
arguments.desc: Max results to return (default: 50, max: 500)

arguments.name: cursor
arguments.desc: Pagination cursor from previous response

name: find_module
description: Search for Python modules by name. Returns module information including file path and symbol counts.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Module name to find. Can be FQN ('myapp.auth') or short name ('auth')

name: list_modules
description: List all Python modules in the indexed project. Returns comprehensive module information.

name: get_callers
description: Find all functions that CALL a given function (reverse call graph / incoming edges). Answer: "Who uses this function?" Results are paginated.

arguments.name: function
arguments.desc: Function to find callers for. Use short name ('login') or FQN ('myapp.auth.login')

arguments.name: limit
arguments.desc: Max results to return (default: 50, max: 500)

arguments.name: cursor
arguments.desc: Pagination cursor from previous response

name: get_callees
description: Find all functions CALLED BY a given function (forward call graph / outgoing edges). Answer: "What does this function depend on?" Results are paginated.

arguments.name: function
arguments.desc: Function to find callees for. Use short name ('process') or FQN ('myapp.payment.process')

arguments.name: limit
arguments.desc: Max results to return (default: 50, max: 500)

arguments.name: cursor
arguments.desc: Pagination cursor from previous response

name: get_call_details
description: Get detailed information about a SPECIFIC call from one function to another. Most detailed view of a single call site.

arguments.name: caller
arguments.desc: The function making the call (short name or FQN)

arguments.name: callee
arguments.desc: The function being called (short name, will match partially)

name: resolve_import
description: Resolve a Python import path to its actual file location in the project.

arguments.name: import
arguments.desc: Import path to resolve. Can be FQN ('myapp.auth.users') or short name ('users')

name: find_dockerfile_instructions
description: Search Dockerfile instructions with semantic filtering. Supports instruction-specific filters (has_digest for FROM, user for USER, port for EXPOSE).

arguments.name: instruction_type
arguments.desc: Filter by instruction type (FROM, RUN, USER, EXPOSE, COPY, ADD, WORKDIR, etc.)

arguments.name: file_path
arguments.desc: Filter by Dockerfile path (supports partial matching)

arguments.name: base_image
arguments.desc: Filter FROM instructions by base image name (e.g., 'python', 'alpine')

arguments.name: port
arguments.desc: Filter EXPOSE instructions by port number

arguments.name: has_digest
arguments.desc: Filter FROM instructions by digest pinning (true=pinned, false=unpinned)

arguments.name: user
arguments.desc: Filter USER instructions by username

arguments.name: limit
arguments.desc: Maximum results to return (default: 100)

name: find_compose_services
description: Search docker-compose services with filtering. Supports configuration filters (has_privileged, has_volume, exposes_port).

arguments.name: service_name
arguments.desc: Filter by service name (supports partial matching)

arguments.name: file_path
arguments.desc: Filter by docker-compose.yml path

arguments.name: has_privileged
arguments.desc: Filter by privileged mode (true=privileged containers only)

arguments.name: exposes_port
arguments.desc: Filter services exposing specific port

arguments.name: has_volume
arguments.desc: Filter services with specific volume path (e.g., '/var/run/docker.sock')

arguments.name: limit
arguments.desc: Maximum results to return (default: 100)

name: get_dockerfile_details
description: Get complete breakdown of a Dockerfile with all instructions and multi-stage analysis.

arguments.name: file_path
arguments.desc: Path to Dockerfile (required)

name: get_docker_dependencies
description: Retrieves dependency information for Docker entities (compose services or Dockerfile stages).

arguments.name: type
arguments.desc: Entity type: "compose" for docker-compose services or "dockerfile" for Dockerfile stages

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Entity name: service name (for compose) or stage name/alias (for dockerfile)

arguments.name: file_path
arguments.desc: Optional: Filter to specific file path

arguments.name: direction
arguments.desc: Traversal direction: "upstream", "downstream", or "both" (default: "both")

arguments.name: max_depth
arguments.desc: Maximum traversal depth (default: 10)
